strategy_research: {"strategies": [{"name": "Mean Reversion with VIX Spike Filter", "description": "Enter long SPY/QQQ when VIX spikes above a Z-score threshold (>+1.5 SD over 20-day mean), confirming a fear-driven oversell. Exit after VIX reverts or within a fixed 3\u20137 day window. Optional enhancement: require VIX futures backwardation (spot > front-month) to confirm panic peak before entry.", "evidence": ["Requejo (2024, SSRN #4708400): SPY/QQQ backtest 1996\u20132022 with walk-forward analysis, Sharpe up to 2.85 on RSI-2/QQQ variant", "VRP Contrarian Strategy (2021\u20132024): avg annual return 20.79%, Sharpe 1.40 via exploiting overestimated volatility risk premium", "Price Action Lab long-only MR (2021\u20132024): CAGR 5.4%, max DD 9.2%, Sharpe 0.80", "ScienceDirect (2024): confirms inverse relation between VIX ETP flows and VIX levels \u2014 mean-reversion operationally exploitable"], "historical_sharpe": "0.80\u20132.85 depending on implementation; VRP-based variant ~1.40; pure RSI-2 variant up to 2.85", "market_conditions": "Works: discrete volatility spikes from political events, earnings shocks, short-lived liquidity crises. Fails: prolonged bear markets (2008\u20132009, 2022) where VIX stays elevated for months; structural breaks like Feb 5 2018 (VIX +115% intraday)", "instruments": ["SPY", "QQQ", "TLT", "AGG"], "timeframe": "daily signal, 3\u20138 day average holding period", "complexity": "medium"}, {"name": "Dual Momentum GEM (Antonacci)", "description": "Monthly rotation among three assets: SPY, EFA, AGG. Step 1 \u2014 absolute momentum: if SPY 12-month return < T-bill rate, go 100% AGG. Step 2 \u2014 relative momentum: if SPY beats T-bill, compare SPY vs EFA over 12 months and hold the winner. Always 100% in one asset.", "evidence": ["Antonacci (2012, CMT Founders Award; 2014 book): 40-year backtest ~17.4% CAGR vs 11.9% S&P 500, max DD ~22% vs ~51%", "Geczy & Samonov (2015): momentum outperformed buy-and-hold back to 1801 across diversified geographic indices", "Antonacci (2023, Medium): updated backtest \u2014 12-month lookback outperformed composite by +120 bps/year over 68 years", "ReSolve Asset Management (2019): proposed ensemble lookback to reduce single-point fragility"], "historical_sharpe": "0.87 (1974\u20132013 backtest); ~0.55 (2002\u20132024 ETF-based); ~0.45 post-publication (2014\u20132024 live)", "market_conditions": "Works: sustained trending markets, prolonged bear markets (2000\u20132002, 2008\u20132009 crash avoidance). Fails: whipsaw markets with rapid reversals (Q4 2018), strong bull markets where it lags equity-only returns, post-publication alpha decay", "instruments": ["SPY", "EFA", "AGG"], "timeframe": "monthly", "complexity": "low"}, {"name": "Vigilant Asset Allocation (Keller VAA)", "description": "Breadth momentum with a canary-asset defensive trigger. Compute 13612W momentum score (12\u00d7r1 + 4\u00d7r3 + 2\u00d7r6 + r12)/4 for SPY, EFA, EEM, AGG. If ANY single asset has negative momentum, shift 100% to the best defensive asset (LQD, IEF, or SHY). Otherwise hold the top-ranked offensive asset.", "evidence": ["Keller & Keuning (2017, SSRN #3002624): VAA backtest 1970\u20132017, Sharpe ~1.10, max DD <15% vs 40%+ for equal-weight benchmark", "Keller & Keuning (2018, SSRN #3212862): Defensive Asset Allocation (DAA) extension with canary universe", "AllocateSmartly live tracking (2018\u20132024): confirmed breadth trigger effectiveness with some post-paper performance decay", "QuantConnect community (2018\u20132023): validated implementation with note on high turnover tax drag"], "historical_sharpe": "~1.10 (VAA-G4 with AGG in offensive universe, 1970\u20132017); ~0.83 without AGG; ~0.90 for VAA-G12", "market_conditions": "Works: clear sustained trends, early regime shifts (caught 2000\u20132002, 2008\u20132009, COVID-19 March 2020). Fails: V-shaped recoveries (re-enters after much of rally is gone), 2022 where both equities and defensive bonds fell simultaneously, high-turnover range-bound markets", "instruments": ["SPY", "EFA", "AGG"], "timeframe": "monthly", "complexity": "medium"}, {"name": "Cross-Asset Momentum + 200-Day Trend Filter + Volatility Targeting", "description": "Three-layer system: (1) Rank SPY, QQQ, IWM, GLD, TLT, EFA, AGG by composite 1/3/6/12-month return score, select top 3\u20135. (2) Apply 200-day SMA trend gate \u2014 exclude assets below their SMA, shift weight toward GLD/TLT/AGG when SPY is below its 200-day. (3) Size each position so it contributes a target daily volatility (0.5\u20131% portfolio), scaling down when aggregate vol exceeds target.", "evidence": ["Moskowitz, Ooi & Pedersen (2012, JFE): time-series momentum Sharpe ~1.16 across diversified asset classes", "J.P. Morgan / CME Group (2015): cross-asset EMV momentum Sharpe ~0.78, 1972\u20132014", "Research Affiliates (2024): vol targeting improves Sharpe for equities and credit; momentum beats buy-and-hold for SPY/IWM, trend-following beats for GLD/TLT", "Xu, Li, Singh & Park (2023, SSRN #4424602): Improved Cross-Asset Time-Series Momentum (I-XTSM) with further performance enhancements", "Man Group (2019\u20132024): vol scaling dramatically reduces crash risk and improves Sharpe for risk assets"], "historical_sharpe": "0.65\u20131.16 depending on universe and vol target; diversified long-term trend-following ~1.16; ETF-sector variant 0.60\u20130.72", "market_conditions": "Works: sustained cross-asset trends with moderate inter-asset correlation. Particularly robust over multi-decade periods. Fails: 2022-type environments where equities, bonds, and most alternatives decline together; whipsaw markets triggering false 200-day SMA exits; low-vol periods where leverage builds then crashes violently", "instruments": ["SPY", "QQQ", "IWM", "GLD", "TLT", "EFA", "AGG"], "timeframe": "monthly rebalance, weekly volatility recalculation", "complexity": "high"}, {"name": "Regime-Based Rotation: VIX + Yield Curve Matrix", "description": "Two-signal regime engine: VIX level (below 15 = risk-on, 15\u201325 = neutral, above 25 = risk-off, above 35 = crisis) crossed with yield curve slope (10Y\u20132Y spread: positive/widening = expansion, negative = recession signal). Each of the four regime quadrants maps to a predefined allocation across SPY/QQQ/IWM (risk-on) vs TLT/AGG/GLD (risk-off). Enhanced version adds MOVE/VIX ratio as early-warning signal.", "evidence": ["Shu, Yu & Mulvey (2024, arXiv 2402.05272): Jump Model regime detection improves Sharpe from 0.46 to 0.78, CAGR from 9.82% to 12.55% (1990\u20132023)", "MDPI Mathematics (2025): VIX dominates as regime signal for equities/commodities; yield curve slope dominates for bonds/real estate; JM-MPC outperforms equal-weight", "SOA (2025): MOVE/VIX-driven dynamic allocation \u2014 14.03% CAGR (2014\u20132025) vs 11.5% S&P 500, only 2 down years (2018, 2022)", "Nature Scientific Reports (2025): LSTM + regime switching Sharpe 1.38 (2017\u20132022 out-of-sample); 187% higher Sharpe in VIX>=25 regimes vs traditional risk parity"], "historical_sharpe": "0.78\u20131.38; rule-based Jump Model ~0.78; MOVE/VIX model ~1.05+; ML-enhanced ~1.38", "market_conditions": "Works: clear macroeconomic regime transitions (2008\u20132009, COVID-19 March 2020, early 2022). Fails: record 25-month yield curve inversion (2022\u20132024) with no recession and SPY +46%; slow-grinding bear markets where VIX spikes lag the top; 2022 simultaneous equity+bond drawdown", "instruments": ["SPY", "QQQ", "IWM", "EFA", "TLT", "AGG", "GLD"], "timeframe": "monthly allocation, weekly signal monitoring", "complexity": "medium"}, {"name": "Hierarchical Risk Parity (HRP, Lopez de Prado 2016)", "description": "Three-step algorithm: (1) Cluster all 8 ETFs by correlation into a dendrogram. (2) Quasi-diagonalize the covariance matrix so correlated assets are adjacent. (3) Recursively bisect the tree, allocating capital at each node in inverse proportion to sub-portfolio variance. No matrix inversion required \u2014 robust to singular/ill-conditioned covariance matrices.", "evidence": ["Lopez de Prado (2016, SSRN #2708678, Journal of Portfolio Management 42(4)): Monte Carlo shows HRP improves out-of-sample Sharpe vs CLA by 31.3%; HRP out-of-sample variance 72.47% lower than CLA", "ScienceDirect (2025): applied to S&P 500 constituents 2005\u20132023, lower risk indicators vs traditional methods", "ResearchGate (2020): HRP achieves higher Sharpe than MPT with lower standard deviation in cross-study comparisons", "Community benchmarks (Quantopian, 2018\u20132023): CLA concentrates 92.66% in top 5 assets vs HRP 62.57% \u2014 demonstrably better diversification"], "historical_sharpe": "+31.3% improvement vs CLA (Markowitz) out-of-sample; comparable to or slightly below equal-weight on raw Sharpe, but with meaningfully lower variance and drawdown", "market_conditions": "Works: multi-asset portfolios where traditional MVO would over-concentrate; unstable correlation regimes where matrix inversion-based methods break down. Fails: concentrated bull markets where being in the single best asset would dominate (e.g. SPY-only in 2023\u20132024); small universes under 8 assets where clustering advantage is minimal", "instruments": ["SPY", "QQQ", "IWM", "VTI", "GLD", "TLT", "AGG", "EFA"], "timeframe": "monthly", "complexity": "high"}, {"name": "VIX Term Structure Contango/Backwardation Signal", "description": "Compute daily VIX roll = (front-month VIX futures \u2013 spot VIX) / business days to settlement. In contango (roll > +0.10): short volatility via VXX or sell VIX futures, hedged with long SPY. In backwardation (roll < \u20130.10, 3+ days confirmed): contrarian long SPY as panic-peak signal. Size positions with hard stop-losses given catastrophic tail risk.", "evidence": ["Simon & Campasano (2014): shorting VIX futures in contango is 'highly profitable and robust' to transaction costs; term structure has forecast power for VIX futures returns", "Wang et al. (2024, PLOS ONE #e0302289): 4 of 7 ML models achieve IR > 0.02; avg C-MVO information ratio 0.623 using term structure features", "Cboe Insights (2022): inverted VIX curve has significant positive relation with subsequent S&P 500 returns (2010\u20132017)", "Quantpedia VRP implementation: Sharpe 0.63; VIX is in contango ~80% of the time since 2010"], "historical_sharpe": "0.63\u20130.78 (conservative implementations); significantly higher in low-vol periods (2013\u20132017, 2019, 2021); catastrophic drawdowns in Feb 2018 and Mar 2020", "market_conditions": "Works: sustained low-volatility regimes (~80% of time); earns persistent roll yield in contango. Fails catastrophically: sudden vol explosions \u2014 Feb 5 2018 (VIX +115% in one session, XIV/SVXY destroyed); March 2020 (VIX hit 85.47). Strategy is inherently short-gamma: small consistent gains, rare catastrophic losses", "instruments": ["SPY", "QQQ"], "timeframe": "daily", "complexity": "high"}, {"name": "Intermarket Divergence: Gold vs. Bonds Regime Signal", "description": "Track 20-day trend direction of GLD and TLT. When they diverge: GLD up + TLT down signals inflationary regime (overweight IWM/GLD, underweight TLT); TLT up + GLD down signals deflationary regime (overweight TLT, reduce equities/GLD). When both fall simultaneously, move to short-duration cash (SHY/BIL). Use GLD/TLT price ratio slope as a continuous macro thermometer.", "evidence": ["ResearchGate (~2014\u20132017): intermarket divergence strategy performed without re-optimization 17 years after original model development", "Global Macro Monitor (2017): GLD and TLT move in same direction ~80% of time over 20-day windows \u2014 divergences are meaningful regime signals", "CXO Advisory (2020): QQQ:IWM vs GLD:TLT switching strategy 6.7% CAGR (2004\u20132020), net of frictions", "VanEck (2024): gold outperforms during deep equity drawdowns; safe-haven bid is idiosyncratic from bond behavior"], "historical_sharpe": "~0.50\u20130.70 estimated; underperforms SPY on net basis (6.7% vs 8.8% CAGR in one study) but provides superior drawdown protection in inflationary regimes", "market_conditions": "Works: clearly defined inflation or deflation regimes with sustained macro trends (2000\u20132009). Fails: 2022 rate shock where TLT fell 31.4% and GLD fell 0.3% simultaneously; rapid V-shaped divergences that reverse before signals trigger; low-dispersion regimes (2017\u20132019) where GLD and TLT move in lockstep", "instruments": ["GLD", "TLT", "SPY", "IWM", "AGG"], "timeframe": "weekly", "complexity": "low"}], "recommendation": "Start with Regime-Based Rotation (VIX + Yield Curve Matrix) for a moderate-risk $1000 account. Among the strategy family preference of mean_reversion_vix_filter, it most directly applies VIX as a regime signal rather than a pure entry trigger, which reduces the catastrophic tail risk of short-vol approaches while preserving VIX's proven predictive power. The rule-based version (no ML required) achieves Sharpe ~0.78\u20131.05 with a clear allocation matrix across your full 8-instrument universe. The SOA (2025) MOVE/VIX implementation delivered 14.03% CAGR vs 11.5% for S&P 500 with only 2 down years (2014\u20132025). Implementation is practical at small scale: only free data needed (VIX from CBOE, 10Y-2Y spread from FRED), monthly rebalancing keeps transaction costs near zero on $1000, and no derivatives or leverage are required. As a second step, layer in the Cross-Asset Momentum + Trend Filter once familiarity with multi-asset rotation is established \u2014 combining momentum ranking with the VIX regime gate creates a stacked-signal ensemble (momentum + regime) that research consistently shows outperforms either signal alone and directly addresses the 2022 failure mode by having GLD as a legitimate regime allocation."}
